market_summary: S&P 500 at ~7,064 (April 21 close), roughly flat for the week (+0.3% from April 17 close of ~7,041). Iran re-closed the Strait of Hormuz on April 18-19 after the US kept its naval blockade, reversing the brief April 17 reopening. Ceasefire expires April 23 with nuclear talks collapsed — Iran refuses to end uranium enrichment. IEA: largest supply disruption in global oil market history. March CPI confirmed +3.3% YoY (+0.9% MoM, largest monthly jump since June 2022), driven by gasoline +18.9% and fuel oil +44.2%. Fed holding at 3.50-3.75%; FOMC April 28-29 at 99.4% probability of no change. JPMorgan sees no cuts in 2026 (possible 25bps hike Q3 2027). Headline CPI forecast to peak at 3.6% in April-May before easing to 3.0% December 2026. IT sector at 31.9% of S&P 500 weight, recovering as tech leads. Energy YTD +22% but rotation underway. NVIDIA Vera Rubin confirmed in full production (BofA), combined order backlog doubled to $1T. AMZN Q1 earnings April 29 with double upgrades from BofA and KeyBanc.
macro_themes: 1. IRAN/HORMUZ: Re-closure April 18-19 is the dominant macro risk. Ceasefire expires April 23. Nuclear talks collapsed — Iran refuses to dismantle enrichment. Stagflation and recession risk elevated. 2. INFLATION: March CPI +3.3% YoY, peaking at 3.6% forecast April-May; core CPI 2.6%. Fed pinned with 99.4% hold probability at April 28-29 FOMC. JPM: no cuts in 2026. 3. AI CAPEX SUPERCYCLE: NVDA Vera Rubin in full production (H2 2026 confirmed BofA), $1T combined order backlog (doubled from $500B). Hyperscalers committing $600B+ capex in 2026. 4. GOLD: XAU structurally bid — Goldman $5,400 year-end target, JPM $5,055 Q4 average, BNP Paribas $6,000. Central banks buying 755-850 tonnes in 2026. PBOC 16 consecutive months of reserve building. 5. SECTOR ROTATION: IT at 31.9% of S&P 500, capital rotating from energy/commodities toward technology. Energy YTD +22% but facing Hormuz volatility headwinds.

CSPX gap at 33.96% vs 36% target ($83 gap) must close by May 3. AMZN and NVDA both carry STRONGER thesis flags ahead of major catalysts (AMZN earnings April 29, NVDA earnings May 20). WHT structure excellent: $3.33 withheld YTD, 6 of 7 positions carry 0% effective WHT. Action cascade this week: ASML exit → CSPX add → FOMC → AMZN earnings → MA earnings → May 3 deadline.

new_opportunities.rationale: Broadcom dominates custom AI silicon (Google TPU, Meta AI accelerators) and hyperscaler networking switches. Confirmed multi-year partnerships (Meta through 2029, Google through 2031). AI chip revenue doubling in 2026. Mizuho $480 target vs ~$390 current = 23% upside. Zero meaningful WHT (negligible dividend). Complements NVDA with custom silicon and networking exposure. Gated: NAV must exceed $5,000 AND CSPX at/above 36% AND ASML fully exited.

Micron is the leading US HBM (High Bandwidth Memory) producer — every NVIDIA Blackwell and Vera Rubin GPU requires HBM, making MU a direct beneficiary of the $1T NVDA order backlog. Up ~168% YTD in 2026, confirming structural AI memory demand. Negligible dividend = near-0% WHT. A 10-15% pullback from current elevated levels would be a disciplined entry point.

action_items: 1. CRITICAL (April 22-25): Exit ASML full position (0.1739 shares, ~$256.76 gross). Thesis DETERIORATING: China mix 36% to 19%, bipartisan DUV restriction bill advancing (JPM warns -10% EPS), stock -6% on Q1 beat. Do not wait for Q2 confirmation.
2. URGENT (immediately post-ASML exit): Deploy $150-170 into CSPX to close 36% NAV target gap. Single transaction, commission-efficient. CSPX is the zero-WHT highest-conviction use of proceeds.
3. IMPORTANT (April 28-29 FOMC): If dovish signal or explicit 2026 cut window mentioned, execute U03A stage-down: sell $100 of U03A and buy CSPX. If hawkish or unchanged, hold U03A in full.

4. IMPORTANT (April 29, 5:30 PM ET — AMZN Q1 earnings): Monitor AWS growth (consensus 28-30%, must exceed 25%), EBIT vs $20.7B consensus, Q2 guidance. Beat with raised guidance = review +$50-75 AMZN add from remaining deployable cash.
5. IMPORTANT (April 30 — MA Q1 earnings): ADD $100 if cross-border volume >14% AND VAS growth >20% AND guidance maintained or raised. HOLD if in-line. Reassess immediately on miss or guidance cut.
6. MAY 3 HARD DEADLINE: Any remaining deployable cash (post-ASML, MA add, FOMC action) into CSPX unconditionally. Retain $50 minimum cash buffer. No extensions.

7. ONGOING — Monitor Iran ceasefire April 23 expiry: GLD 4-week risk-on invalidation clock does NOT start unless Hormuz is formally and durably reopened with a peace deal — ceasefire expiry without formal deal does not trigger. Current: HOLD GLD unconditionally.
8. WATCH (May 20): NVDA Q1 FY2027 earnings — key catalyst for Vera Rubin production ramp confirmation and $78B Q1 revenue guidance execution. No pre-earnings action.
